ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR CO.'S MOTION TO DISMISS FOR DELAY IN PROSECUTION PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 583.310 AND 583.360. NO APPEARANCE AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DENIED. DEFENDANT REPRESENTED THAT PLAINTIFF IS IN PROPIA PERSONA. DEFENDANT FAILED TO PROVIDE PROPER NOTICE PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 1005(B), WHICH INCREASES THE 16-COURT-DAY NOTICE PERIOD BY 10 DAYS WHEN SERVICE IS BY MAIL TO A LOCATION OUTSIDE OF CALIFORNIA BUT WITHIN THE UNITED STATES. Notice Of Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Prosecution
Off Calendar Mar-15-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR CO.'S MOTION TO DISMISS FOR DELAY IN PROSECUTION PURSUANT TO CCP 583.310 AND 583.360.
Appearances: J. Paul Fanning of Lankford Crawford Moreno LLP (925.300.3520) for defendant and moving party; no other appearances.
The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted. No opposition filed. Order signed. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Action For Delay In Prosecution
Off Calendar Jan-19-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Ford Motor Co.'s motion to dismiss for delay in prosecution pursuant to C.C.P. 583.310 and 583.360 is denied. Defendant represented that plaintiff is in propia persona. Defendant failed to provide proper notice pursuant to C.C.P. 1005(b), which increases the 16-court-day notice period by 10 days when service is by mail to a location outside of California but within the United States. The proof of service demonstrates that defendant served plaintiff by mail on December 19, 2011 to an address in Nevada. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported = (503/TLJ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Prosecution
Off Calendar May-06-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Sharone L. Clisham, Esq. of WALSWORTH FRANKLIN BEVINS & McCALL (415-781-7072), appearing for defendant / moving party QUINTEC INDUSTRIES, INC.

RULING:
Defendant Quintec Industries's motion to dismiss for delay in prosecution is granted. No opposition filed. The complaint was filed in 2003, over 5 years ago. A hearing is required unless defendant's counsel advises the plaintiff of the tentative ruling and the plaintiff states that he does not wish to contest the tentative ruling. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
